topic: Case study: document classification — TF-IDF vs. contextual embedding
scope: Designed vs. learned, head to head (modality-specific leakage: vectorizer fit before split, duplicate/same-author docs across folds).
key_concepts: A direct comparison of a pipeline using designed representations (TF-IDF) and one using learned representations (SBERT).
Both pipelines terminate in a standard classifier (e.g., Logistic Regression) to isolate the impact of the representation.
The risk of vectorizer leakage: fitting the TF-IDF vocabulary on the entire dataset before making the train-test split.
Modality-specific text leakage: inadvertently placing duplicate documents or documents from the exact same author in both training and validation splits.
examples: Building two scikit-learn pipelines: one extracting TF-IDF features, another extracting frozen SBERT embeddings, and comparing their classification accuracy on the same dataset.

relations.prerequisites: TF-IDF (s02), sentence embeddings (s04), and pipeline leakage concepts (Ch 2).
relations.builds_toward: The global pipeline design synthesis in Ch 17.

exclusions: Deeply tracing the SBERT tensor outputs or coding the full model.
Evaluating deep neural network classifiers (keep the classifier simple to evaluate just the feature representations).
